title: Was String Theory Created To Stagnate Physics?
summary: This interview segment discusses the unusual connection between Edward Witten, a leading string theorist, and his father, a prominent anti-gravity researcher from the 1950s. The conversation explores whether string theory may have been developed as a deliberate dead-end to stagnate physics progress.

key_claims.claim: Edward Witten, considered the world's most brilliant physics mind of our time, is the son of the country's most prominent anti-gravity researcher of the 1950s
String theory allowed physics to proceed as if it was doing something new while breaking no new ground in the physical world
Top physicists working on unconventional projects were connected to graphics businesses
